- Opens a dropdown/menu/popover and queries for content (getByText, getByRole)
- Uses querySelector with OUIA selectors to interact with components
- Asserts on element roles, aria attributes, or data attributes that changed
If any test would break due to your changes, fix it. Common test fixes:
- Wrap assertions in waitFor() when content renders via portal
- Add popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }} to component renders in tests
- Update getByRole/getByText queries to match new attribute values

Apply ALL of the following 6 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 193
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: primary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Code context:
```
  192                  data-test="task-cta"
  193                  variant={ButtonVariant.primary}
  194                  className="opp-quick-search-details__form-button"

```

### Fix 2
Line: 190
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: SplitItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  189              <SplitItem>
  190                <Button
  191                  isDisabled={isTektonHubTaskWithoutVersions(selectedItem)}

```

### Fix 3
Lines: 183, 224, 261, 276
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-label]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Label> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Label> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Label variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Label>
  After:  <Label variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Label> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Label><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Label>
  After:  <Label icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Label>

Incident context:
  componentName: Label
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: LevelItem

Strategy: LlmAssisted

Component <SplitItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <SplitItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:

  After:  <SplitItem component=>other children</SplitItem>

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/console-plugin/source/src/components/task-quicksearch/PipelineQuickSearchDetails.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the main file and then the test file.

Now let me reason through each fix:

### Fix 1 (Line 193): `Button.variant` type changed
The value `ButtonVariant.primary` is still valid in the new type (`'primary'` is in both old and new). **No change needed.**

### Fix 2 (Line 190): Icon children to prop for Button
Looking at the Button (lines 190-207), it contains text children via `{getCtaButtonText(selectedItem, selectedVersion)}` — no icon children. The rule says to move icon content to the `icon` prop, but there's no icon in this Button. **No change needed.**

### Fix 3 (Lines 183, 224, 261, 276): Icon children to prop for Label
- Line 183: `<Label data-test="task-provider">{selectedItem.provider}</Label>` — text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 224-230: `<Label color="green" icon={<CheckCircleIcon />} ...>` — icon is **already** passed via the `icon` prop (already migrated). **No change needed.**
- Line 261-267: `<Label color="blue" ...>{category}</Label>` — text only, no icon. **No change needed.**
- Line 276-278: `<Label color="blue" ...>{tag}</Label>` — text only, no icon. **No change needed.**

### Fix 4 (Lines 189, 210): SplitItem `component` prop
The `component` prop on `SplitItem` is for specifying the HTML element type (e.g., `'div'`, `'span'`, `'li'`), similar to other PatternFly layout components. It's NOT for moving children content into it. The current children are complex components (Button, PipelineQuickSearchVersionDropdown) that should remain as children. **No change needed.**

### Fix 5 (Lines 255, 273): StackItem `component` prop
Same as Fix 4 — `component` prop is for specifying the HTML element type, not for absorbing children. The current children (LabelGroup) should remain as children. **No change needed.**

### Fix 6 (Line 13): Replace `TextContent` with `Content`
This is a genuine migration. `TextContent` is deprecated and should be replaced with `Content`. I need to:
1. Replace `TextContent` with `Content` in the import
2. Replace `<TextContent ...>` and `</TextContent>` with `<Content ...>` and `</Content>` in the JSX

Now let me apply the changes:
Now let me verify the final file looks correct:

The file looks correct. Now let me review the test file for any issues caused by my changes.

The only change I made was replacing `TextContent` with `Content`. Looking at the test file:

- The tests use `data-test` attributes (`queryByTestId`) — not affected by the `TextContent` → `Content` change
- No test imports or references `TextContent` directly
- No test queries for `TextContent` by role, tag, or text
- The `Content` component renders the same DOM structure (a `<div>` by default), so `data-test="task-description"` will still work
- No dropdown/menu/popover interactions that would need portal handling for this change

The test file requires **no changes**.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The value `ButtonVariant.primary` (`'primary'`) is valid in both the old and new type union. The only change was the addition of `'stateful'`, which doesn't affect existing usage. |
| Fix 2 | Icon children to prop for `Button` | **No change needed** | The Button at line 190 contains only text children (`{getCtaButtonText(...)}`), not icon children. No icon to move to the `icon` prop. |
| Fix 3 | Icon children to prop for `Label` | **No change needed** | All four Label instances either contain only text children (lines 183, 261, 276) or already have the icon passed via the `icon` prop (line 224 with `icon={<CheckCircleIcon />}`). No migration needed. |
| Fix 4 | `SplitItem` new `component` prop | **No change needed** | The `component` prop specifies the HTML element type (e.g., `'div'`, `'span'`), not content. The existing children (Button, PipelineQuickSearchVersionDropdown) should remain as children. |
| Fix 5 | `StackItem` new `component` prop | **No change needed** | Same as Fix 4 — the `component` prop specifies the HTML element type. The existing children (LabelGroup) should remain as children. |
| Fix 6 | Replace `TextContent` with `Content` | **Applied** | Replaced `TextContent` with `Content` in the import statement (line 13) and in JSX usage (lines 239, 252). No props needed renaming (`isVisited` was not used). |

The file looks correct. Now let me review the test file for any issues caused by my changes.

The only change I made was replacing `TextContent` with `Content`. Looking at the test file:

- The tests use `data-test` attributes (`queryByTestId`) — not affected by the `TextContent` → `Content` change
- No test imports or references `TextContent` directly
- No test queries for `TextContent` by role, tag, or text
- The `Content` component renders the same DOM structure (a `<div>` by default), so `data-test="task-description"` will still work
- No dropdown/menu/popover interactions that would need portal handling for this change

The test file requires **no changes**.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The value `ButtonVariant.primary` (`'primary'`) is valid in both the old and new type union. The only change was the addition of `'stateful'`, which doesn't affect existing usage. |
| Fix 2 | Icon children to prop for `Button` | **No change needed** | The Button at line 190 contains only text children (`{getCtaButtonText(...)}`), not icon children. No icon to move to the `icon` prop. |
| Fix 3 | Icon children to prop for `Label` | **No change needed** | All four Label instances either contain only text children (lines 183, 261, 276) or already have the icon passed via the `icon` prop (line 224 with `icon={<CheckCircleIcon />}`). No migration needed. |
| Fix 4 | `SplitItem` new `component` prop | **No change needed** | The `component` prop specifies the HTML element type (e.g., `'div'`, `'span'`), not content. The existing children (Button, PipelineQuickSearchVersionDropdown) should remain as children. |
| Fix 5 | `StackItem` new `component` prop | **No change needed** | Same as Fix 4 — the `component` prop specifies the HTML element type. The existing children (LabelGroup) should remain as children. |
| Fix 6 | Replace `TextContent` with `Content` | **Applied** | Replaced `TextContent` with `Content` in the import statement (line 13) and in JSX usage (lines 239, 252). No props needed renaming (`isVisited` was not used). |
